verses.text: Elders come to inquire, but God refuses, bidding the prophet make them know 'the abominations of their fathers.' History must be faced. God will not be sought lightly by those who ignore their sin.
Elders come to inquire, but God refuses, bidding the prophet make them know 'the abominations of their fathers.' History must be faced. God will not be sought lightly by those who ignore their sin.
Elders come to inquire, but God refuses, bidding the prophet make them know 'the abominations of their fathers.' History must be faced. God will not be sought lightly by those who ignore their sin.
Elders come to inquire, but God refuses, bidding the prophet make them know 'the abominations of their fathers.' History must be faced. God will not be sought lightly by those who ignore their sin.
In Egypt He made Himself known and swore to bring them out, yet they rebelled there; still He acted 'for my name's sake.' Grace from the start. God's mercy, not their merit, was their deliverance.
In Egypt He made Himself known and swore to bring them out, yet they rebelled there; still He acted 'for my name's sake.' Grace from the start. God's mercy, not their merit, was their deliverance.
In Egypt He made Himself known and swore to bring them out, yet they rebelled there; still He acted 'for my name's sake.' Grace from the start. God's mercy, not their merit, was their deliverance.
In Egypt He made Himself known and swore to bring them out, yet they rebelled there; still He acted 'for my name's sake.' Grace from the start. God's mercy, not their merit, was their deliverance.
In Egypt He made Himself known and swore to bring them out, yet they rebelled there; still He acted 'for my name's sake.' Grace from the start. God's mercy, not their merit, was their deliverance.
He brought them to the wilderness, gave His statutes and sabbaths as a sign 'that they might know that I... sanctify them.' Gifts of grace. The law itself was a token of His setting them apart.
He brought them to the wilderness, gave His statutes and sabbaths as a sign 'that they might know that I... sanctify them.' Gifts of grace. The law itself was a token of His setting them apart.
He brought them to the wilderness, gave His statutes and sabbaths as a sign 'that they might know that I... sanctify them.' Gifts of grace. The law itself was a token of His setting them apart.
They rebelled in the wilderness; He would have consumed them but wrought for His name's sake. Spared again. God's regard for His own glory preserved an unworthy people.
They rebelled in the wilderness; He would have consumed them but wrought for His name's sake. Spared again. God's regard for His own glory preserved an unworthy people.
He swore they should not enter the land, yet His eye spared them and He made no full end. Tempered judgment. Mercy mingled even with the wilderness sentence.
He swore they should not enter the land, yet His eye spared them and He made no full end. Tempered judgment. Mercy mingled even with the wilderness sentence.
He swore they should not enter the land, yet His eye spared them and He made no full end. Tempered judgment. Mercy mingled even with the wilderness sentence.
He charged their children not to follow their fathers' ways but to hallow His sabbaths and know Him as God. A fresh call. Each generation is summoned to its own obedience.
He charged their children not to follow their fathers' ways but to hallow His sabbaths and know Him as God. A fresh call. Each generation is summoned to its own obedience.
He charged their children not to follow their fathers' ways but to hallow His sabbaths and know Him as God. A fresh call. Each generation is summoned to its own obedience.
The children too rebelled, yet again He withdrew His hand for His name's sake. Patient forbearance. God's long restraint magnifies both His mercy and their guilt.
The children too rebelled, yet again He withdrew His hand for His name's sake. Patient forbearance. God's long restraint magnifies both His mercy and their guilt.

Because they despised His judgments, He swore to scatter them and 'gave them... statutes that were not good' — the fruit of their own choices. Judicial hardening. Persistent sin is punished by being given over to its consequences.

Because they despised His judgments, He swore to scatter them and 'gave them... statutes that were not good' — the fruit of their own choices. Judicial hardening. Persistent sin is punished by being given over to its consequences.

Because they despised His judgments, He swore to scatter them and 'gave them... statutes that were not good' — the fruit of their own choices. Judicial hardening. Persistent sin is punished by being given over to its consequences.

Because they despised His judgments, He swore to scatter them and 'gave them... statutes that were not good' — the fruit of their own choices. Judicial hardening. Persistent sin is punished by being given over to its consequences.
Their fathers blasphemed Him by sacrificing on every high hill; 'what is the high place?' Idolatry exposed. The places of false worship are named for what they are.
Their fathers blasphemed Him by sacrificing on every high hill; 'what is the high place?' Idolatry exposed. The places of false worship are named for what they are.
Their fathers blasphemed Him by sacrificing on every high hill; 'what is the high place?' Idolatry exposed. The places of false worship are named for what they are.
Are they polluted like their fathers, wishing to 'be as the heathen'? That thought 'shall not be at all.' No escape into the world. God will not let His people simply blend with the nations.
Are they polluted like their fathers, wishing to 'be as the heathen'? That thought 'shall not be at all.' No escape into the world. God will not let His people simply blend with the nations.
Are they polluted like their fathers, wishing to 'be as the heathen'? That thought 'shall not be at all.' No escape into the world. God will not let His people simply blend with the nations.
With a mighty hand He will rule them, bring them 'into the wilderness of the people,' and plead with them face to face. Sifting ahead. God deals personally with His people to refine them.
With a mighty hand He will rule them, bring them 'into the wilderness of the people,' and plead with them face to face. Sifting ahead. God deals personally with His people to refine them.
With a mighty hand He will rule them, bring them 'into the wilderness of the people,' and plead with them face to face. Sifting ahead. God deals personally with His people to refine them.
As He pleaded with their fathers in Egypt, so now; He will 'purge out... the rebels' from among them. Separation. Judgment distinguishes the truly His from the merely numbered.
As He pleaded with their fathers in Egypt, so now; He will 'purge out... the rebels' from among them. Separation. Judgment distinguishes the truly His from the merely numbered.
As He pleaded with their fathers in Egypt, so now; He will 'purge out... the rebels' from among them. Separation. Judgment distinguishes the truly His from the merely numbered.
Let them serve their idols if they will, but 'in mine holy mountain' all Israel shall serve Him, and there He will accept them. Pure worship promised. God reserves a people who will truly honour Him.
Let them serve their idols if they will, but 'in mine holy mountain' all Israel shall serve Him, and there He will accept them. Pure worship promised. God reserves a people who will truly honour Him.

He will accept them 'with your sweet savour' and be sanctified in them; they shall know the LORD when He deals with them for His name's sake. Grace, not merit. Their restoration glorifies His mercy, not their goodness.

He will accept them 'with your sweet savour' and be sanctified in them; they shall know the LORD when He deals with them for His name's sake. Grace, not merit. Their restoration glorifies His mercy, not their goodness.
A parable of the southern forest set ablaze, every face scorched; the people scoff, 'Doth he not speak parables?' Word dismissed. Those who will not heed mock the very form of God's message.
